function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'usage' to reach function 'main'?
Is it impossible for function 'rand' to reach function 'usage'?
loop_reachability: Can the loop with condition 'i<n' be skipped entirely?
Is the loop with condition 'i<n' always executed?
Is it possible that the loop with condition 'j<n' is never executed?
Is the loop with condition 'j<n' always executed?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 25:  <statement> return -1; </statement> is reached, the statement line 29:  <statement> for(i=0; i<n; i++) </statement> has already been reached?
Is it guaranteed that whenever line 39:  <statement> int l=rand()%n; </statement> is reached, the statement line 28:  <statement> int matriz[n][n]; </statement> has already been reached?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable l at line 39: <statement> 				int l=rand()%n; </statement> depend on the value of variable n at line 27: <statement> 		n=atoi(argv[1]); </statement>?
Does variable argc at line 10: <statement> int main(int argc, char* argv[]) </statement> determine the value stored in variable contador at line 14: <statement> 		int contador=0; </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'nome' remain in use at the end of function 'usage'?
Is variable 'fp' live at the end of function 'main'?
dead_code: Can the statement 'FILE* fp=fopen("grafo.txt", "w");' be considered dead code?
